change_summary: Shadow-audited a liquid 63-day base-breakout universe definition without changing production tickers or strategy code.
hypothesis: A narrow shadow universe can surface replayable low-overlap candidates with useful forward returns without adding production tickers.
notes: Artifact: data/experiments/exp-20260428-020/exp_20260428_020_narrow_shadow_universe_definition.json. Definition: liquid non-ETF fresh close above prior 63-day high after >=30 trading days without a prior 63-day high, 20d return <=18%, volume ratio >=1.10, close location >=0.60. Candidates 13/10/18 in late_strong/mid_weak/old_thin; same-day A/B overlap 7.69%/0.00%/5.56%; outside-production candidates 0. Forward 10d avg -5.63%/+4.26%/+4.38%; aggregate weighted 10d +1.71% with 63.16% win rate. Observed_only: do not promote because late_strong is negative and fixed snapshots have survivorship/production-universe coverage limits. Do not repeat archived-news event scouts on the same sparse archive or OHLCV base-breakout promotion without an external survivorship-aware universe source.
